briefdescription.para: < Public API

detaileddescription.para: Get attached view. Empty handle if this visual is not be attached.

simplesect.para: The attached view, or empty handle if not be attached.

briefdescription.para: Get the attached container range type. INVALID if this visual is not be attached.

simplesect.para: The attached container range type, or INVALID if not be attached.

inbodydescription.para: < Use Internal::VisualBase::GetInternalContainerRangeType() if we are internal API

briefdescription.para: Detach from the attached view.
Perform an action on a visual registered to this view. Visuals will have actions, this API is used to perform one of these actions with the given attributes.

simplesect.para: If visual is not been registered to the view, action should be ignored.

parameterdescription.para: The action to perform. See Visual to find supported actions.

briefdescription.para: Get the type of this VisualBase.

simplesect.para: The type of this VisualBase

briefdescription.para: Retrieves a property value.

simplesect.para: BaseHandle is not subclass of Handle. So this API is not use Handle.SetProperty
The property value
This returns the value set by SetProperty() or the animation target value if it is being animated.

briefdescription.para: Convenience function for obtaining a property of a known type.

simplesect.para: The property value
The property types match i.e. PropertyTypes::Get<T>() is equal to GetPropertyType(index).

briefdescription.para: Sets the value of an existing property.

simplesect.para: BaseHandle is not subclass of Handle. So this API is not use Handle.SetProperty

parameterdescription.para: The new value of the property

briefdescription.para: Gets the name of the VisualBase.

simplesect.para: The name of the VisualBase

briefdescription.para: Sets the name of this VisualBase.
Gets the color of the VisualBase.

simplesect.para: The color of the VisualBase

briefdescription.para: Sets the color of this VisualBase.
Gets the X offset of the VisualBase.

simplesect.para: The X offset of the VisualBase

briefdescription.para: Sets the X offset of the VisualBase.
Gets the Y offset of the VisualBase.

simplesect.para: The Y offset of the VisualBase

briefdescription.para: Sets the Y offset of the VisualBase.
Gets the width of the VisualBase.

simplesect.para: The width of the VisualBase

briefdescription.para: Sets the width of the VisualBase.
Gets the height of the VisualBase.

simplesect.para: The height of the VisualBase

briefdescription.para: Sets the height of the VisualBase.
Gets the proportion flags of the VisualBase.

simplesect.para: The proportion flags of the VisualBase

briefdescription.para: Sets the proportion flags of the VisualBase.
Gets the extra width of the VisualBase.

simplesect.para: The extra width of the VisualBase

briefdescription.para: Sets the extra width of the VisualBase. This width will be added to final width absolutly, independent as proportion flags.
Gets the extra height of the VisualBase.

simplesect.para: The extra height of the VisualBase

briefdescription.para: Sets the extra height of the VisualBase. This height will be added to final height absolutly, independent as proportion flags.
Gets the origin of the VisualBase.

simplesect.para: The origin of the VisualBase

briefdescription.para: Sets the origin of the VisualBase.
Gets the pivot of the VisualBase.

simplesect.para: The pivot of the VisualBase

briefdescription.para: Sets the pivot of the VisualBase.
Gets the corner radius of the VisualBase.

simplesect.para: The corner radius as Vector4 (topLeft, topRight, bottomRight, bottomLeft)

briefdescription.para: Sets a uniform corner radius for all four corners.

simplesect.para: Only supported type of visual has efforts.

parameterdescription.para: The corner radius to apply to all corners

memberdef.argsstring: (float topLeft, float topRight, float bottomRight, float bottomLeft)
memberdef.name: SetCornerRadius

briefdescription.para: Sets individual corner radii for all four corners.

simplesect.para: Only supported type of visual has efforts.

parameterdescription.para: The radius for the top-left corner
The radius for the top-right corner
The radius for the bottom-right corner
The radius for the bottom-left corner

briefdescription.para: Sets corner radii from a Vector4.

simplesect.para: Only supported type of visual has efforts.

parameterdescription.para: Corner radii as Vector4 (x=topLeft, y=topRight, z=bottomRight, w=bottomLeft)

briefdescription.para: Gets the corner radius policy.

simplesect.para: The corner radius policy

briefdescription.para: Sets the corner radius policy.

simplesect.para: Only supported type of visual has efforts.

parameterdescription.para: ABSOLUTE for world-unit values (default), RELATIVE for percentage [0.0, 0.5] of the shorter side

briefdescription.para: Shortcut to set the corner radius policy to RELATIVE.

detaileddescription.para:
Only supported type of visual has efforts.
Equivalent to SetCornerRadiusPolicy(CornerRadiusPolicy::RELATIVE).

briefdescription.para: Returns true if the corner radius policy is RELATIVE.

simplesect.para: True if the policy is RELATIVE, false if ABSOLUTE

briefdescription.para: Gets the corner squareness of the VisualBase.

simplesect.para: The corner squareness as Vector4 (x=topLeft, y=topRight, z=bottomRight, w=bottomLeft)

briefdescription.para: Sets a uniform corner squareness for all four corners.

simplesect.para: Only supported type of visual has efforts.

parameterdescription.para: The squareness value to apply to all corners

memberdef.argsstring: (float topLeft, float topRight, float bottomRight, float bottomLeft)
memberdef.name: SetCornerSquareness

briefdescription.para: Sets individual corner squareness values for all four corners.

simplesect.para: Only supported type of visual has efforts.

parameterdescription.para: The squareness for the top-left corner
The squareness for the top-right corner
The squareness for the bottom-right corner
The squareness for the bottom-left corner

briefdescription.para: Sets corner squareness from a Vector4.

simplesect.para: Only supported type of visual has efforts.

parameterdescription.para: Squareness values as Vector4 (x=topLeft, y=topRight, z=bottomRight, w=bottomLeft)

briefdescription.para: Gets the borderline width of the VisualBase.

simplesect.para: The borderline width

briefdescription.para: Sets the borderline width of the VisualBase.

simplesect.para: Only supported type of visual has efforts.

briefdescription.para: Gets the borderline color of the VisualBase.

simplesect.para: The borderline color

briefdescription.para: Sets the borderline color of the VisualBase.

detaileddescription.para:
Only supported type of visual has efforts.
If the UiColor has a color ID, it is resolved from the current theme and a binding is registered so the color is automatically refreshed when the theme changes.

briefdescription.para: Gets the borderline offset of the VisualBase.

simplesect.para: The borderline offset

briefdescription.para: Sets the borderline offset of the VisualBase.

simplesect.para: Only supported type of visual has efforts.

briefdescription.para: Assigns this VisualBase instance to a target variable. This method is useful for capturing a reference to a VisualBase created within a declarative UI tree for later use.
Executes a custom action on this View instance.

detaileddescription.para: Useful for performing additional setup (e.g. signal connections, predefined style application) without breaking the method chain.

parameterdescription.para: A callable (lambda or free function) invoked with a reference to this instance.

simplesect.para: Reference to this View for fluent chaining.

briefdescription.para: Sibling Order Change API.

detaileddescription.para: Get the sibling order of the visual object inside of the container.

simplesect.para: The sibling order inside of the container. Or 0 if visual object is not in container.

briefdescription.para: Set the sibling order of the visual object inside of the container.

simplesect.para: It will change other VisualBase's sibling order to keep the order.
It will throw assert if siblingOrder is bigger than the number of visuals that contianer has.

parameterdescription.para: The sibling order inside of the container.

briefdescription.para: Raise the visual object above the next sibling visual object.

simplesect.para: The VisualBase has been initialized.
The VisualBase has been parented.

briefdescription.para: Lower the visual object below the previous sibling visual object.

simplesect.para: The VisualBase has been initialized.
The VisualBase has been parented.

briefdescription.para: Raise visual object above all other sibling visual objects.

simplesect.para: The VisualBase has been initialized.
The VisualBase has been parented.

briefdescription.para: Lower visual object to the bottom of all other sibling visual objects.

simplesect.para: The VisualBase has been initialized.
The VisualBase has been parented.

briefdescription.para: Raises the visual object above the target visual object.

simplesect.para: The VisualBase has been initialized.
The VisualBase has been parented.
The target visual object is a sibling.

briefdescription.para: Lower the visual object to below the target visual object.

simplesect.para: The VisualBase has been initialized.
The VisualBase has been parented.
The target visual object is a sibling.

briefdescription.para: This constructor is used by Dali New() methods.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to a newly allocated Dali resource

briefdescription.para: VisualBase is a owner of Visual::Base.

detaileddescription.para: It represents the base visual object that can be attached to a Dali::Ui::View. VisualBase manages attachment/detachment, sibling order, and property updates. A VisualBase can belong to only one View's ContainerRangeType; adding it to another View or ContainerRangeType will automatically remove it from the previous one.
VisualBase could change sibling order. It will change the rendering order at view. Sibling order only works at same type of ContainerRangeType. VisualBase could not over the container.
For example, if ContainerRangeType added at BETWEEN_BACKGROUND_AND_CONTENT, this visual could not be rendered under the background, or over the content.

VisualBase origin and pivot define the reference point of the visual relative to its attached view. Origin define where should the Pivot positions from attached view. (Similar with ParentOrigin and Pivot property of View class.)

Offset properties (X/Y) are expressed as a proportion of the pivot from the origin. Width/Height By default, Offset and Size parameters are proportions by attached view size. You can set Dali::Ui::Visual::Transform::ProportionFlags to change each varaibles be absolute or relative.
ExtraWidth/ExtraHeight is additional absolute size of the visual. (Could be negative value) The extra size expand from the Pivot.
VisualBase itself don't have constructor. We should construct class by subclass of VisualBase, e.g. ColorVisual::New().

simplesect.para: VisualBase properties applied before LayoutController process. So if you try to create, or change properties during Layout, (e.g. ColorVisual::New() at OnMeasure()) that VisualBase properties didn't applied at this phase, so flickering will be occured. DO NOT create or change VisualBase properties during Layout calculation.
